ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of A Supplemental Accounting For The Reserve
Off Calendar Dec-07-2010 Continued To Apr-05-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Vaughn Korbin for and with Charles Jonas.

The Court addressed notice to the Minor beneficiaries. Mr. Korbin stated the Minor beneficiaries are actually adults and that they all were served through their attorney. The Court stated that it would allow the service. Mr. Jonas addressed the fee request stating that 10% of it will go to his accountant, that it wasn't his intent to include some of the statutory work, that the real estate had to be cleaned up, that he saved the estate some money, that the tax work was complex, that he was dealing with five German beneficiaries, that it was complicated work, and that he doesn't think the extraordinary fees were out of line. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
